The train arrived at Leavcnworth
at , o'clock this morning and was
sent back to Spokane bringing with
il all the passengers and the United
States mail. which will be sent over
the Northern Pacific.


It is with great difficulty that the
Northern Pacific is able to handle its
trains, having found it necessary to
run a snowplow q few minutes in ad-
vance Of their trains. Freight is at
stardstill on all lines in this sec
ton.


Three large wrccking crews are on
the way to the crippled bridge west
of the Cascades on the Great North
ern.


Special instructions have been is
sued not to accept live stock ship
ments or to sell tickets for points
between Leavenworth and Seattle.


The only trains now running be-
tween Spokane and Leavenworth are
the two local trains. The eastbound
train viii be handled as usual. but
with some difficulties which will be
overcome promptly.


Superintendent w. y. Scott has
been fighting the snow blockade be-
tween Old Mission and Leavcnworth
